KUMIHO · GRAPH-NATIVE COGNITIVE MEMORY

Smarter memory,
no chunking required

Version-controlled graph memory and belief revision for AI agents — kumiho-server · kumiho-memory · kumiho-benchmarks, and arXiv:2603.17244.

Kumiho · for engineers & technical decision-makers · 2026
THE QUESTION

Slicing documents into pieces —
does it actually improve accuracy?

We answer with code, benchmarks, and a paper. Spoiler — chunked RAG 29.8%, Kumiho 93.3%.

1
ACT 01 / 05 · BASICS
What is
chunking?
CHUNKING 101
Before critiquing it, understand it precisely — what chunking is, and why it became the default.
ACT 1 · CHUNKING 101
Chunking — the standard recipe: split, embed, retrieve
STEP 1

Split

Cut docs into few-hundred-token pieces

→
STEP 2

Embed

One vector per chunk

→
STEP 3

Store

Index in a vector DB

→
STEP 4

Retrieve

top-k chunks nearest the query

→
STEP 5

Inject

Paste into the LLM prompt

Retrieval itself works fine. The trouble starts after the text becomes chunks — relationships, identity, and history are gone.
ACT 1 · LIMITS
For a long-lived agent, chunks hurt in four places
1
IdentityChunks are anonymous — no address, no lifespan, no relationships
2
History & provenanceNowhere to attach versions or provenance
3
Token economicsCost grows with every token of context
4
Human workflowApproval and publishing live outside the retrieval loop
So the question — instead of matching pieces at read time, what if memory had structure from the start?
2
ACT 02 / 05 · STRUCTURE
Kumiho's
skeleton
GRAPH-NATIVE SKELETON
Every memory is a graph citizen with an address (kref) — Item → Revision → Artifact.
ACT 2 · MODEL
Cognitive memory and assets share one graph skeleton
address kref://project/space/item.kind?r=3&a=image A permanent address for every memory & asset
↓
nodes
Itemidentity
Revisionimmutable snapshot
Artifactfile pointer
Edgesemantic link
↓
COGNITIVE MEMORYbelief · conversation
Beliefs, conversations, decisions — same bones
summaryimplicationseventsSUPERSEDES
CREATIVE ASSETSimage · model …
Version control for models, images, sequences
DEPENDS_ONDERIVED_FROMCREATED_FROMREFERENCED
↓
Revision lifecycleIMMUTABLE + TAGS
New Revision
stacked, append-only
→
SUPERSEDES
auto-linked by server
→
latest moves
system-managed tag
→
published
one-way · frozen
Tag moves keep time-range history — point-in-time (time-travel) resolution
ACT 2 · VERIFIED
Turns out — retrieval was already revision-centric
TYPICAL CHUNK RAG
WHAT KUMIHO'S CODE DOES
Embeddings live
on anonymous chunks
directly on Revision nodes
Search
vector similarity alone
hybrid UNION — vector index + fulltext
Embedding text
the full raw text
title·summary·description only — no "vector dilution"
Broad signals (entities·facts) go to fulltext instead — what was missing wasn't "revision-centric search", it was everything above and below it.
ACT 2 · WHAT'S LEFT
So what was actually missing?
① Citizenship for chunks

Anonymous pieces → addressable nodes

  • skill_ingest makes an Item+Revision per ## section
  • Re-ingest = a new revision stacked on the same Item
② Automated summaries

summary used to be hand-written

  • LLM structured summaries, generated
  • implications & events come along
③ Hierarchical lazy retrieval

Summaries first, source if needed

  • Summary mode is the default
  • Blobs load lazily
The hard part is ingestion — an Artifact is a location pointer, not content, so fetching and processing the bytes is the real pipeline.
3
ACT 03 / 05 · REALITY
Where chunks
belong
WHERE CHUNKS BELONG
Chunking earns its keep in exactly one place — the text projection.
ACT 3 · LAYERS
Chunking is valid in one layer only — the text projection
Source artifact
Any file format — EXR, geometry caches, audio…
fetch the blob directly
Markdown projection
Derived text view of the artifact
one blob = read it whole
Chunks
Embedded, summarized pieces — addressable nodes
semantic = just what you need
Splitting binaries creates no meaning — the target of chunking is the markdown projection.
ACT 3 · MISCONCEPTION
"Chunks read faster" — mostly wrong
chunks = fast reads
→
chunks = selective retrieval
Re-stitching pieces from a graph is slower than reading one blob. The real value: the two paragraphs you need instead of a 40KB document — token savings. Need the whole thing? Just read the markdown blob.
4
ACT 04 / 05 · THE ALTERNATIVE
Structure,
at write time
WRITE-TIME ENRICHMENT
Instead of matching at read time — build summaries, implications, events, and edges the moment you store. And beliefs get revised.
ACT 4 · kumiho-memory 0.5.3
Build everything at write time — six stages
skill_ingest
## section → Item+Rev
→
summarize
structured · full model
→
implications
3–5 future cues · light · parallel
→
events
[when] event → consequence
→
discover_edges
auto edges to related memories
→
dream_state
nightly consolidation
match at read time
→
structure at write time
Summary (full model) and implications (light model) run in parallel — implications are appended as "Future relevance:" and vector-indexed with the summary.
ACT 4 · MECHANISMS
Four mechanisms that do chunking's job — better
1
Prospective indexingPre-index 3–5 "moments months later when this matters" — in different vocabulary, closing the query–cue gap
2
Event extraction[when] event → consequence — keeps the causality that narrative compression drops
3
Sibling filteringUnfiltered siblings balloon context 10–30× — the LLM reranker keeps only the best 1–3
4
reflect — the agent's own LLMThe host agent decides what to remember — no external API key needed
Chunking bets on word matching; these bet on meaning and structure.
ACT 4 · BELIEF REVISION
Belief revision — AGM as graph operations
Contradiction detection belongs to the LLM layer (write-time assess + Dream State) — the server provides the mechanical guarantees: SUPERSEDES · latest · the deprecated filter.
ACT 4 · FORMAL GROUNDS
Seven postulates — 49 of 49 scenarios pass
K*2 Success
New information is always incorporated
49/49 PASS
K*3 · K*4
Inclusion · Vacuity — change no more than needed
PASS
K*5 · K*6
No coexisting contradictions · syntax-independent
PASS
Hansson ×2
Relevance · Core-Retainment — unrelated memories untouched
PASS
Recovery is rejected on principle — with immutable versioning, history always survives, so a "recovery" postulate is unnecessary (the paper's stance). 49/49 = operational adherence.
5
ACT 05 / 05 · EVIDENCE
By the
numbers
EVIDENCE
Same judge, same criteria — 401 LoCoMo-Plus questions.
ACT 5 · LOCOMO-PLUS
Chunk retrieval comes last — 1M tokens can't catch up
System
Approach
Context
Accuracy
RAG
Chunk retrieval (embed-large)
top-k pieces
29.8%
Mem0 family
Mem0 · A-Mem · SeCom
memory systems
41–43%
GPT-4.1
Full-context stuffing
entire conversation
43.6%
Gemini 2.5 Pro
Full-context stuffing
1M tokens
45.7%
Kumiho
no chunking summaries + graph
250–400 tokens
93.3%
93.3% = 374/401 (answers by GPT-4o) · recall accuracy 98.5% is answer-model-invariant · swapping only the answer model (4o-mini ~88% → 4o) adds +5.3pp — accuracy is a property of the architecture. Baselines as reported by the LoCoMo-Plus paper.
ACT 5 · TOKEN ECONOMICS
Summaries compress the source 40–280×
Raw replayk=5 injected
50,000+ tokens
Summary modek=5 injected
250–400tokens
simple fact42× profile50× planning session278× full 401-question run~$14 graph query80–120ms
Ratios are representative cases from the deployed graph (paper) — summaries win on cost and latency, not just accuracy.
ACT 5 · WHY
Why summaries beat full context
FULL CONTEXT IN PRACTICE

Just a bigger whiteboard

  • Gemini at 1M tokens still lands at 45.7%
  • "…write more at once, but erased between sessions" (§1)
  • External research: lost-in-the-middle · distractors
SUMMARIES + GRAPH

Nothing to hallucinate from

  • The graph holds no fabricated text — nothing to hallucinate from
  • Sibling filter blocks the 10–30× balloon
  • Only the right 250–400 tokens go in
"Cognitive memory is not a context capacity problem but an organization, enrichment, and retrieval problem" — arXiv:2603.17244 §15.3
ACT 5 · MEASURED
What the measurements established
+31.7pp
Enrichment ablation61.6% → 93.3%
+47pp
>6-month gaps37.5% → 84.4% — cliff removed
98.5%
Recall accuracy395/401 · answer-model-invariant
97.5%
Adversarial refusalLoCoMo n=446
27 failures = 21 fabrication · 6 recall weakest: goal type85% LoCoMo F1, 4-category0.447 overall incl. adversarial0.565 independent repromid-80%
Open homework, stated openly — goal reasoning, a controlled summary-vs-full comparison (for publication, n≈30–50), and tens-of-millions-node scale.
Stop matching at read time.
Build structure at write time.
Summaries, implications, events, and edges are born at store time, and beliefs are revised the AGM way. Chunking isn't discarded — it goes in the drawer, a tool reserved for very large single documents.
Kumiho · Graph-Native Cognitive Memory · arXiv:2603.17244
